sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 21 - Guideline for Calculation of CRVM Reserves When (b) is Greater Than (a).pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 21 - Guideline for Calculation of CRVM Reserves When (b) is Greater Than (a)

observedSectionWindows.note: Title, calculation rule text, and background information on the `(a)` / `(b)` comparison.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 21 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 22 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded.
Treat the guideline as review-only reserve guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: CRVM reserves when `(b)` is greater than `(a)` and rules for determining `(a)`
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: One-page AG 21 CRVM-reserves guideline

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, permitted treatment, or required reserve-calculation language in the `(a)` / `(b)` comparison context as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat Standard Valuation Law, 1980 CSO Tables, modified net premiums, net level premium, and full preliminary term method as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the `(a)` / `(b)` comparison and the zeroing of a negative excess as reserve structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the way the guideline frames the reserve comparison as calculation structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the `(a)` / `(b)` notation and modified-net-premium language as context until the text clearly instructs a calculation step.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, appendix-style mortality tables, or the full preliminary term method as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-batch page window as a review-only boundary that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the noisy OCR layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat the page-image wording as the backstop and do not widen the batch because OCR looks uncertain.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 21 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to page 1 only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 21 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-page / short-guideline checks for future compact guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Source-bound reserve-context checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag21-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-099 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 21 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 21 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 21 planning artifact. Planned batch 099 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
